calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Elliott for Randall Dick (Trustee). Bruce Methven for Marie Gardyne and Carl Conti.

The Parties stated that they have not engaged in discovery. The Petition for Instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-08-2014 Continued To Sep-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Continued To Sep-08-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Elliott for (Trustee) Randall Dick. Bruce Methven for and with Marie Gardyne and for Carl Conti.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether the pecuniary bequests are valid, provided history, explained chain of events, etc. Counsel are to file responses/points and authorities to the probate examiners notes by September 3, 2014. The petition for instructions was continued to September 8, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and will be deemed under submission on that date unless the parties are asked to come in and argue.

Judge: Andrew Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Demurrer To The City And County Of San Francisco'S Second Amended Petition To Recover Trust Property Or, Alternatively, For Permission To File Late Creditor'S Claim; Or Alternatively, To Impose Contructive Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-14-2011 Continued To Mar-21-12 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Ammons for CCSF (Petitioner). Robert Elliott for Randall Dick (Respondent/Trustee).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the alter ego issue. The City has filed a Civil Complaint against the Estate of Samuel Conti. Mr. Ammons stated that it appears that the Estate of Samuel Conti has not been open in San Francisco County and raised the issue of venue. The Hearing on the Demurrer was continued to March 21,2012 at 2:30 p.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing on THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S SECOND AMENDED PETITION, etc. is set for March 28, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: N/A.

Demurrer To The City And County Of San Francisco'S Second Amended Petition To Recover Trust Property Or, Alternatively, For Permission To File Late Creditor'S Claim; Or Alternatively, To Impose Contructive Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-14-2011 Continued To Dec-14-11 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Ammons for City and County of San Francisco. Robert Elliott for Randall Dick (Trustee).

The Court put the matter over for 60 days for the Parties to go back to the Civil Department to establish alter ego. The Demurrer was continued to December 14, 2011 at 2:30 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

The City And County Of San Francisco'S Petition To Recover Trust Property Or, Alternatively, Impose Constructive Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court previously sustained the demurrer with leave to amend on or before June 29, 2011. Nothing has been filed and there was no appearance by any Party. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Demurrer To The City And County Of San Franciscos Amended Petition To Recover Trust Property Or, Alternatively, Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Off Calendar

The City And County Of San Francisco'S Petition To Recover Trust Property Or, Alternatively, Impose Constructive Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-25-2011 Continued To Jun-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Ammons for City and County of San Francisco. Robert Elloitt Randall Dick (Trustee/Respondent).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the probate Court having jurisdiction to determine whether Mr. Conti is the alter ego of Fascination, whether this issue should be a civil matter, whether it should be pursued as an unlawful detainer or a separate complaint, and whether the City's petition to file late claim should be allowed. The Demurrer is sustained with 30-days leave to amend to plead additional facts with respect to late discovery and an opportunity to see if something further can be alleged with regard to the alter ego. Counsel agreed to engage in settlement discussions. The matter was continued to June 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Elloitt is prepare an order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien.
